anti_claim: Cognitive operator registry rows validate a public operator taxonomy and dogfood receipt shape only; they do not authorize live operator execution, source mutation, release, provider calls, private-data equivalence, account/session export, or whole-system correctness.

public_private_boundary.public_inputs: public operator ids, role classifications, source refs, validator refs, dogfood receipt metadata, negative-case ids, and copied non-secret macro source-module bodies with hashes and anchors

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A prose-only operator glossary, action router, or automation surface that lacks runnable validation, dogfood receipts, negative cases, source-module manifests, and public/private boundaries.
failure_modes: operator row admitted without a role, purpose, or public runtime reference
dogfood receipt accepted without validator refs or observed negative-case coverage
cognitive operator registry treated as live operator execution authority
registry projection used to authorize source mutation, release, provider calls, or account/session access

private operator thread body, raw voice body, provider payload body, prompt shelf body, secret value, account/session state, browser/HUD live access, or credential-equivalent material exported through public receipts
source module manifest missing, target body digest stale, or declared anchor absent
receipt embeds copied macro body text instead of carrying public refs, hashes, counts, and verdicts
synthetic fixture row counted as operator coverage without the source-open macro registry, standard, and validator body imports
standard_payload.ontology: A public cognitive-operator registry organ that validates operator ids, roles, runtime refs, dogfood receipts, negative cases, secret-exclusion policy, and copied non-secret macro registry/standard/validator bodies.
standard_payload.teleology: Show how the macro system names reusable cognitive operations without leaking private operator material or pretending a registry card can execute, mutate, or release work.

validation_rules: Every operator row must carry an id, role, purpose, and public runtime reference.
Every dogfood receipt must cite validator refs and observed negative-case coverage.
Exported runtime bundle must include source_module_manifest.json and copied non-secret macro body modules whose target hashes and anchors validate.

Private operator bodies, raw voice bodies, provider payload bodies, account/session state, live browser/HUD access, credential material, release authority, provider-call authority, source-mutation authority, and whole-system correctness claims are rejected.
Receipts must carry refs, hashes, counts, verdicts, and omission boundaries without embedding copied macro body text.
